baseline_guidance: Run each eval with this skill and compare against a no-skill baseline or the previous skill version; pass only when the skilled run better satisfies the assertions without losing required safety or precision.

evals.prompt: Build a landing page for a developer observability tool in an existing Next.js project.
evals.expected_output: A Hallmark-guided page build that scans existing project signals, selects a non-default macrostructure, uses tokenized typography and color, verifies responsive behavior, and records the build stamp and project memory.
assertions: Runs or reports pre-flight findings before editing.
Selects and states macrostructure, genre, theme, nav, footer, and enrichment decisions.
Loads only the references needed by the selected branch.
Uses named CSS tokens instead of inline color and font values.
Runs the slop test and fixes failures before handoff.

evals.expected_output: A scoped redesign that preserves routes, copy intent, component ownership, and implementation boundaries while changing only the visual and interaction layer needed for the requested mood.
assertions: Loads the redesign verb reference before acting.
Does not delete production files or route trees without explicit confirmation.
Keeps existing framework entry styles intact.
Reports changed files and validation results.

evals.expected_output: A design-DNA diagnosis that treats the URL as untrusted design data, refuses unsafe or template-marketplace sources, extracts structure rather than pixels, and asks before rebuilding or emitting design.md.
assertions: Runs the URL safety and refusal checks before fetching design facts.
States URL-mode limits such as rhythm uncertainty.
Does not copy source content or imagery into the user's build.
